question:
Will the price of Bitcoin be above $84,000 on December 1?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

evidences.title: BITCOIN Closed 2 straight green 1M candles after 7 months! - Binance
evidences.content: Image 1: Binance Square Login Sign Up DiscoverNewsNotificationProfileBookmarksChatsHistoryCreator CenterSettings Article BITCOIN Closed 2 straight green 1M candles after 7 months! Image 2 TradingShot Oct 31, 2024 Follow Report Block User Image 3 Bitcoin (BTCUSD) is closing today the monthly (1M) candle and unless it drops by 7000 in a few hours, it will close the month of October in green. That will be the 2nd straight green 1M candle since March! This 7 month consolidation period is no stranger to BTC as such patterns, where there are no straight green 1M candles, are standard Accumulation Phases that we see during Bull Cycles. So far on the current one we've had three (including March 2024) and once the market closed 2 straight green 1M candles, it rallied. [...] The 2019 - 2021 Bull Cycle had three such straight green candle occasions and a very clear Accumulation Phase, while the 2015 - 2018 Cycle had numerous. One thing is clear based on this multi-year chart. When the market closes two straight green 1M candles, it is always a good signal to buy. But what do you think? Do you find this indicator reliable? Are you buying based on this? Feel free to let us know in the commen...

evidences.title: 4 US Economic Events to Shake Bitcoin Sentiment in First Week of December 2025
evidences.content: Fed Interest Cut Probabilities Fed Interest Cut Probabilities. Source: CME FedWatch Tool Conversely, persistent inflation would prompt caution from the Fed, possibly disappointing markets looking for aggressive easing. Consumer sentiment is reported at 10:00 am ET, with the prior value at 51.0 on the economic calendar. This data gauges household views on the economy and spending. Weakening sentiment can signal slowing demand and further support the case for easier monetary policy, which often lifts Bitcoin. These four key economic releases in a single week create a high-stakes environment for digital asset markets. Bitcoin's correlation with traditional risk assets means macroeconomic news is likely to drive market direction more than crypto-specific events. [...] ## PCE Inflation Data Friday, December 5, brings the PCE (Personal Consumption Expenditures) price index at 8:30 am ET, the Fed's preferred inflation benchmark. This report is pivotal, as it tracks progress toward the central bank's 2% goal. It will be released alongside personal income and spending data, providing a comprehensive view of consumer health. Investors will focus on both headline and core PCE numbers. A so...

evidences.content: ### Instructions 1. Select an exchange and choose one or more cryptocurrency pairs (for example BTCUSD, ETHUSD or XRPUSD). By moving the mouse over each pair you can see how much historical data is available for download. 2. Select at least one candlestick timeframe such as 1 minute, 5 minutes, hourly or daily bars. CryptoDatum provides historical OHLCV data from 1m up to 1d. 3. Choose your preferred format (CSV, Excel or ForexTester). Format samples are shown in the section below. 4. Pay only for the datasets you selected (5.99 € per dataset – VAT included). 5. Wait while we prepare a ZIP archive containing your historical crypto datasets. 6. Download your datasets using the provided link (it will also be sent to your email). ### What data is included in the datasets? evidences.title: December Bitcoin Rally: Fed Just Triggered Bitcoin’s Mega Cycle!
evidences.content: In my opinion, this is the core reason why Bitcoin has begun to rally. Bitcoin responds to macro turning points before they're fully recognized by the broader market. This first move in December was about the market pricing a shift in liquidity conditions. The market is transitioning out of this restrictive regime into an environment where liquidity is either neutral or positive. Neutral at worst, positive at best. So, I think this is bullish for Bitcoin. Historically, we've seen that this kind of shift leads to explosive Bitcoin price action because it is a multimonth, multi-year regime shift. This is where we're at now. The conditions that have historically supported coin's most powerful moves, they are forming again and the asset is already signaling what could come next. My name is [...] is the dynamic that happened in 2020 and 2021 when we saw the Fed expand the balance sheet aggressively. We saw Bitcoin tend to repric upward with extraordinary speed. And the reason is simple. Bitcoin is the highest sensitivity asset to global liquidity. When liquidity surges, Bitcoin absolutely leaps higher. This has been demonstrable over Bitcoin's history. Now, I'm not saying this is gua...

evidences.title: A sudden $13.5 billion Fed liquidity injection exposes a crack in the dollar that Bitcoin was built for
evidences.content: If the Dec. 1 spike fades and repo usage returns to low levels, it suggests the system just needed dollars for mechanical reasons. If these operations repeat and SOFR holds above target, or if the Standing Repo Facility gets more active, then the signal tilts toward tightening. Bitcoin reacts very differently across those two regimes: one fosters relaxed risk-taking, the other drains it. Right now, the market sits in a delicate equilibrium. ETF flows have cooled, yields have steadied, and liquidity is uneven heading into year-end. A $13.5 billion repo doesn’t rewrite that picture, but it slots neatly into it, showing a system that isn’t strained enough to worry but not loose enough to ignore. And that’s where Bitcoin comes in. [...] Senior Analyst • CryptoSlate 5 min read A giant golden Bitcoin coin bursts upward in front of the Federal Reserve building under a bright blue sky, symbolizing crypto markets reacting to the Fed’s $13.5B repo liquidity jump. Cover art/illustration via CryptoSlate. Image includes combined content which may include AI-generated content. Results for macro news are, however, not sensitive to the window size. The sample is from January 2017 to December 2022. evidences.title: Bitcoin rally now depends on one Fed document coming Wednesday
evidences.content: On-chain flows add a further caution. Whale-sized deposits to exchanges reached about 49,000 BTC as the price reclaimed $60,000, increasing the supply available to sell into any post-minutes strength. Options positioning concentrates around the same zone, with dealer gamma clustered at $60,000 and $62,000, levels that can either pin the price or accelerate a slide depending on which way it breaks. Holding the $62,000 area after the minutes would keep the recovery intact, and a move through Monday's high near $64,700 would confirm it. A slide back toward $58,000 would mark the jobs-driven bounce as a failed rally inside a bear market that began with October's $126,198 record high. [...] However, the jobs report changed that. Beyond the headline miss, the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) revised April and May payrolls down by a combined 74,000 jobs, and the unemployment rate's dip to 4.2% came only because roughly 720,000 people left the labor force, pulling participation down to 61.5%. Traders responded by pushing hike expectations later: CME FedWatch pricing now implies about a 76% chance the Fed holds at its July 28-29 meeting, with roughly 40% odds of an increase by December....
